title: Building Makaverse — My Internship Journey in VR Development
author: Joy Abrian
summary: A reflection on my internship experience at Diskominfo Makassar, where I contributed to the Makaverse project by developing VR features, implementing avatars, and building interactive multiplayer systems.
content:
During my internship at Diskominfo Kota Makassar, I had the opportunity to work on an ambitious project called Makaverse — a virtual environment representing the city of Makassar. This experience became a significant milestone in my journey as an Information Systems student focusing on Virtual Reality.

---

[1] Entering the World of Makaverse

Makaverse is a metaverse-based project initiated to digitally represent Makassar City. As part of the development team, I was involved in building interactive VR features that allow users to explore and engage within a virtual space.

This project is not only technical but also cultural, as it aims to bring local identity into a digital environment.

---

[2] Developing VR Features in Unity

My primary responsibility was developing VR functionalities using Unity. I worked on creating interactive environments where users can move, explore, and interact naturally.

This includes optimizing performance to ensure the VR experience remains smooth and accessible.

---

[3] Avatar Integration with Ready Player Me

One of the most interesting parts of the project was implementing character avatars using Ready Player Me. I handled importing, configuring, and integrating 3D avatar models into Unity.

This allowed users to have personalized representations of themselves داخل the Makaverse environment, increasing immersion and engagement.

---

[4] Real-Time Communication System

To enhance social interaction, I developed and integrated a real-time voice chat system. This feature allows players to communicate directly within the virtual environment.

Implementing this system required careful consideration of synchronization and performance, especially in a multiplayer setting.

---

[5] Multiplayer Interaction

Makaverse is designed as a shared space. I contributed to implementing multiplayer functionality, enabling multiple users to interact simultaneously within the same virtual world.

This includes handling player synchronization, positioning, and interaction logic across clients.

---

[6] Animation and Asset Optimization

I also worked on creating and configuring character animations for VR interactions. Additionally, I optimized 3D assets to ensure performance efficiency without sacrificing visual quality.

This step was crucial in maintaining a balance between realism and performance in VR.

---

[7] Lessons Learned

This internship taught me more than just technical skills. I learned how to:
- Work in a real development team
- Adapt to project requirements and constraints
- Balance performance and user experience in VR
- Communicate ideas effectively within a team

---

Conclusion

My internship at Diskominfo Makassar was a valuable experience that strengthened my skills in VR development and real-time systems. Working on Makaverse gave me insight into how virtual environments can be used to represent real-world cities and communities.

This journey also reinforced my interest in combining technology, interaction, and storytelling — something I continue to explore in my projects today.

title: The Last Alibi — Full Development Journey (From Concept to Implementation)
author: Joy Abrian
summary: Comprehensive documentation of the development of The Last Alibi, an AI-driven investigative game, from the initial concept to the optimization of its dialogue system and dynamic narrative generation.
content:
The Last Alibi is a narrative-driven investigative game that I developed as part of my thesis in the field of Information Systems. The main focus of this project is how to integrate AI into gameplay to create a dynamic and non-repetitive investigative experience.

---

[1] Initial Concept and Core Gameplay

In the early stage, I designed gameplay where the player takes on the role of a detective who must interrogate multiple suspects. Each suspect has their own story, and the player's task is to find contradictions in their statements.

Instead of using static dialogue, I chose a procedural narrative approach, where stories and clues are generated dynamically. This makes each play session feel unique.

---

[2] Dialogue System and Typewriter Effect

To enhance immersion, I developed a turn-based dialogue system where NPCs respond to the player gradually. Each response is displayed using a typewriter effect to make it feel more alive and realistic.

I also added loading text to simulate the NPC "thinking," especially since the responses are generated by AI.

---

[3] AI Integration (Generative Dialogue)

One of the core features in The Last Alibi is the use of AI to generate dialogue and character backstories. I used a prompt engineering approach to ensure each NPC has a consistent personality.

Each character includes:
- Name
- Personality traits
- Unique backstory
- Hidden clues

The AI not only generates stories but also embeds contradictions that players must analyze.

---

[4] Clue System and Difficulty Scaling

To maintain gameplay balance, I implemented a difficulty system:
- Easy: 3 clues
- Normal: 2 clues
- Hard: 1 clue

Clues are embedded implicitly within the narrative rather than presented explicitly. This forces players to pay close attention to dialogue details.

---

[5] Interrogation UI/UX

I designed the interrogation interface to focus on text and character expression. The UI is minimalistic, with an emphasis on readability.

Key elements include:
- Main dialogue text
- Input field for player questions
- Visual feedback when NPCs respond

This approach keeps players focused on the analytical process.

---

[6] AI Optimization and Efficiency

One of the biggest challenges was optimizing AI API usage. To address this, I:
- Avoided using JSON (used delimiters like '|' and '\n' instead)
- Stored NPC data locally after generation
- Reduced the number of requests by maintaining conversational context

This approach successfully reduced token usage while maintaining narrative quality.

---

Conclusion

The Last Alibi is not just a game, but also an exploration of how AI can be used to create more adaptive and personalized gameplay experiences.

Through this project, I learned a lot—from game design and AI integration to optimization. Moving forward, I plan to further develop this system, including potential implementation on web and VR platforms.

This project also demonstrates that AI can be utilized not just as a supporting tool, but as a core part of gameplay.
